source_pr.title: planner: push TopN down when N is less than a specific variable
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #26089 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

Problem Summary: planner: push TopN down when N is less than a specific variable

### What is changed and how it works?

Whether push `TopN` down is determined by cost, but due to inherent inaccurate estimation, `TopN` would not be pushed down sometimes. Several issues about this were found, so to mitigate this problem, we decided to introduce a new variable `tidb_opt_limit_push_down_threshold` and push `TopN` down forcibly when `N < this-var`.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- planner: push TopN down when N is less than a specific variable

root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: tk.MustQuery(`explain format=brief select a from t order by a desc limit 10`).Check(testkit.Rows( (planner/core/integration_test.go).
